key_points: Campus locked down since protests; gates closed to public and alumni
Over-enrollment by 300+ students strains already-tight space
DHS detained a student inside a residential building on campus
key_quotes: They wouldn't let me show my 4yo daughter the campus.
They over-enrolled 300 students this year, you have enough money to hire more security and open the gates.
My conspiratorial ass immediately thinks the administration is complicit and gave up student housing info.

key_points: Grad students especially struggle to make friends; loneliness is common
Political tension and protest culture dominates campus atmosphere
Some students thrive socially; NYC access helps but campus feels insular
key_quotes: Columbia was the most emotionally exhausting, toxic atmosphere I've ever experienced in my life.
I genuinely have not made a single friend and I'm so bored and lonely all the time.
I miss seeing people skate and bike and families having picnics.

key_points: JJ's dining hall praised by some students as genuinely good
Local restaurants price-gouge families during graduation week aggressively
No widespread dining hall complaints but NYC cost of living hits hard
key_quotes: JJs and their omelettes with guac + burger is the best. Columbia dining food is lit.
They applied a massive event markup, raising a standard $26 menu item to $36 - a 38.46% price hike.
They billed me $10 for water once lmfao.

key_points: Academic pressure crushes students; imposter syndrome is rampant, especially in STEM
Transfer students report health plummeting and feeling they don't belong
Students openly post about depression, burnout, and not doing 'enough'
key_quotes: Every time a grade comes back I have no motivation to keep going, even though I keep pushing harder each time. My health has plummeted.
Honestly getting tired of hearing bad news after bad news from the school.
I graduated back last year from undergrad, and I admittedly hadn't done all that much... I feel like I didn't do enough.

key_points: Financial aid office accused of mismanagement and discrimination in federal lawsuit
Low-income students face brutal NYC cost of living on top of tuition
Tips post for surviving NYC financially went viral among GS students
key_quotes: Someone in SFS did fix it…but someone in SFS unfixed it, dropped the student's classes, and demanded a crazy amount of money.
Columbia is a shady slumlord corporation with a few schools attached.
The financial aid only goes to 12 credits. The people who prioritize graduating in 2 years are the ones who take out more private loans.

key_points: Core Curriculum is genuinely rigorous and intellectually respected by students
STEM median GPA is ~3.2; grade inflation does NOT happen here unlike peer schools
Some Core professors are hostile and create toxic classroom environments
key_quotes: The admin is a mess, but the actual education was fantastic for me.
An advisor told me the STEM average is around a 3.2 at graduation.
She constantly interrupts people mid-sentence, tells them they're 'wrong again' or 'not paying attention,' and has openly said she's 'embarrassed' by the class's first papers.

key_points: Academic advisors described as 'completely useless'; high turnover means no continuity
Admin capitulated to Trump's demands for $200M, drawing widespread alumni disgust
Expansion decisions made without student input; commencement moved off campus without notice
key_quotes: My academic advisor has been the third one assigned to me in four years: I have never been able to establish a real relationship with my advisor.
Harvard held, Columbia folded… Not a good look and not the end.
What moron was involved in this decision? Classic case of 'well I'm fine so you must be too.'

key_points: NYC location is a massive asset for internships, culture, and career access
Campus has been physically locked to the public since 2024 protests
Morningside Heights neighborhood safety concerns; attempted robbery reported near 109th St
key_quotes: As an alumnus who was at Columbia during 9/11 — they never shut down campus. And now, for the first time ever, campus is closed.
I miss seeing people skate and bike and families having picnics.
He wouldn't let go of this lady's bag until me and two women stepped in. Be careful out there.

key_points: Career services widely criticized as weak and unhelpful for pre-professional students
NYC location partially compensates; many pivot to consulting or finance regardless of major
Econ major took 5 years to break $100k; humanities majors realistically expect $40-50k starting
key_quotes: Career services and pre-professional advising are not strong whatsoever.
I was an Econ major at Columbia and it took me ~5 years to break 100k. My job has nothing to do with my major.
For Columbia, many will start their careers outside their majors, like in management consulting, and their first year salaries will be around that $100K mark.

key_points: Ivy tuition plus NYC cost of living is a brutal financial combination
SEAS industrial engineering degree is not ABET accredited — a real professional liability
Endowment at $15.9B yet operating surplus collapsed 63%; students feel fee creep everywhere
key_quotes: Thoughts on college in New York City... does anyone know how much I'd struggle? I know it's a very expensive place to live.
Good luck getting into that chem lab! [re: enrollment expansion with no new resources]
The petition has to include a pledge to never donate to the school.

key_points: Politically charged, high-stress environment that polarizes students strongly
First-gen and low-income students report both struggle and genuine belonging
Campus culture shaped more by controversy and tension than typical college fun
key_quotes: Columbia was the most emotionally exhausting, toxic atmosphere I've ever experienced in my life. People are very accusatory and love to cause drama over nothing.
I'm also a low income first gen student from a high school with a SAT median actually below 1000 and I love it here as well.
Honestly getting tired of hearing bad news after bad news from the school. A little bitter at having worked so hard to get through just for them to speedrun trashing the school reputation in 3 years.

key_points: Administration: capitulated to federal pressure, agreed to $200M settlement, alarming free speech rollback
Security: DHS detained student inside residential building; ICE surveillance reported on campus
Data: applicant personal data including SSN stolen in confirmed hack; second breach disclosed same year
Advising: students lost degrees and post-grad job offers due to advisor errors with no accountability
Accreditation: Columbia formally notified its accreditation is in jeopardy
key_quotes: Who's repaying these loans if they revoke Columbia's accreditation? Not me lmao.
Just got the letter saying my name, dob, ssn, etc. were all part of the data breach. Hooray!
What did Columbia expect when they caved to this regime last year? The bully never steals your lunch money once.

key_points: 'Ignorance' class teaches what science doesn't know yet — legendary, low-stakes, beloved
CULPA professor review database lets students dodge notoriously bad Core instructors
Strong Nobel Prize and Fulbright history; world-class faculty accessible at undergrad level
key_quotes: Ignorance is restarting in Spring 2026, where you learn about what we don't know — the only assessments are one short essay at the end.
Research professors for core & prereq classes. I'm so serious. If all of a professor's CULPA reviews say they're a hardass, drop it while you can.
Columbia is one of the top 32 Fulbright Award institutions in the world, and our Nobel Prizes history is pretty cool.
